entry.title: SF Onboarding → Celonis via API – only few tasks visible
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

We're currently connecting SuccessFactors Onboarding to Celonis via API, but only get:

  • 2 tasks from “Data Collection”
  • 1 out of 6 “New Hire Tasks”

These show up in Celonis, but only partially – e.g., titles, comments, and content are missing. Other tasks (visible in SF under “Manage Data” with Process ID) don’t come through the API at all.

Question:
Has anyone managed to pull more tasks or fields? Any workarounds (OData, Integration Center, Reports etc.)?

Thanks for sharing your experience!

Best Regards,

Svetlana

SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding API OData

entry.title: Error when try to create an Service Binding via ADT in Eclipse Editor
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I'm trying to create a Service Binding (OData V4 - UI type) via ADT in Eclipse Editor connected to a BTP Trial ABAP Environment, but got the below error message. Anyone could guide on how I should proceed to fix this issue?

 
Thanks in advance!
 
Douglas Marques
 
eclipse.buildId=4.32.0.20240606-1231
java.version=22.0.2
java.vendor=Eclipse Adoptium
BootLoader constants: OS=win32, ARCH=x86_64, WS=win32, NL=en_US
Framework arguments:  -product org.eclipse.epp.package.jee.product
Command-line arguments:  -os win32 -ws win32 -arch x86_64 -product org.eclipse.epp.package.jee.product
 
org.eclipse.e4.ui.workbench
Error
Thu Jun 05 14:38:12 BRT 2025
Cannot invoke "com.sap.adt.util.ui.databinding.DatabindingSectionComposite$DatabindingSectionPart.getSection()" because "this.bindingTypeInfoSectionPart" is null
 
java.lang.NullPointerException: Cannot invoke "com.sap.adt.util.ui.databinding.DatabindingSectionComposite$DatabindingSectionPart.getSection()" because "this.bindingTypeInfoSectionPart" is null
at com.sap.adt.cds.servicebinding.ui.internal.editors.ServiceBindingPage.createFormBodyContent(ServiceBindingPage.java:79)
at com.sap.adt.cds.servicebinding.ui.internal.editors.ServiceBindingPage.createFormContent(ServiceBindingPage.java:51)
at org.eclipse.ui.forms.editor.FormPage.lambda$0(FormPage.java:166)
at org.eclipse.swt.custom.BusyIndicator.showWhile(BusyIndicator.java:67)
at org.eclipse.ui.forms.editor.FormPage.createPartControl(FormPage.java:166)

at org.eclipse.ui.forms.editor.FormEditor.setActivePage(FormEditor.java:612)

entry.title: Fiori Custom Fields and Logic app error
entry.content:
Dear experts,

we habe a problem with opening the Custom Fields and Logic App on SAP Fiori Launchpad. Everytime you open the app we got the error message that the system is on write protect mode. We looked in the transaction SCC4 and SE06 to look up if the system is proteceted against changes. But the system (specially) the client is not protected against changes therefore we searched the internet for similar errors and we added the fiori katalog into the roles of the user, but also without any success. 

Could anyone of you help us in this matter, we would be very appreciated. 

Best regards,

Adam

entry.title: How to Integrate SAP Ariba with Third-Party Applications – Standard APIs
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I have a requirement to integrate SAP Ariba (Buyer module) with third-party applications (non-SAP). I would like to know:

  • Are there any standard APIs provided by SAP Ariba for such integrations?

  • If yes, could you please share the list of those APIs or relevant documentation?

  • Is there any recommended approach or best practice for integrating Ariba with external systems?

I’m particularly looking for direct integration options (not via SAP S/4HANA or middleware) if available.

Any guidance or reference to API documentation would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you in advance!
Best regards,
Satheesh Kumar.

entry.title: How to Integrate SAP IBP with Third-Party Systems – Standard APIs
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

We have a business requirement to integrate SAP Integrated Business Planning (SAP IBP) with  other third-party applications.

I would like to know:

  • Are there any standard APIs or integration methods provided by SAP IBP for such use cases?

  • Can we use standard OData, REST APIs, or CPI-based integration for connecting IBP with MRO systems?

  • Is there any recommended architecture or reference content available from SAP for such integrations?

Any guidance, documentation links, or examples of similar integrations would be very helpful.

Thanks in advance!
Best regards,

Satheesh Kumar.

entry.title: SAP MDK Create Media action failing on IOS platform for the Attachment Control.
entry.content:
Hi,

I'm working with the SAP ODATA V2 API (API_CV_ATTACHMENT_SRV) to upload attachments in my MDK application. The attachment is getting uploaded on the Android device, but the same operation is failing on an iOS device. I'm using the Attachment Form Cell Control to upload the attachments, and I have created a CreateMedia OData action to handle the upload. I'm getting the below error when I try to upload an image: 

I have also attached the error log details and the Create Media action details. I hope this helps.

Thank you for your help.

Aditya

entry.title: Duplicated GET requests in odata v4 batch
entry.content:
Hello UI5 experts!

After moving with our apps from 1.208 to 1.120, I started observing strange behaviour in OData V4 batch request to the backend.

I have simple RAP application, using List Report - Object Page floorplan. Object Page is bound to the main entity, and additionally there are a few page fragments with controls bound to different main entity's navigations. When object page renders, OData model sends one big batch containing multiple GET requests - to the main entity and to all sub-entities (from controls added as fragments). So far so good. But for some reason, all GET requests generated from page fragments are duplicated. In general it does not harm anyhow, but it results in some performance issues.

Why is it so? Did anyone observe similar behaviour?

entry.title: ERROR : While using OData adapter to fetch MPL to make report.
entry.content:
Hi @everyone

I hope your doing well.

I am working with OData adapter to fetch MPLs from CPI. Strangely while i am triggering the flow, It is keep on processing state and at last it is going to Abandoned status. 

My analysis on this, While i am trying to fetch less data records i.e appx 1000 it is fine. While i am trying to fetch more it is going in processing.

My requirement, I need to fetch the MPLs and need to sort out the records based on Iflow names(There are 45 Iflows, It will be given dynamically what ever is needed.) and TimeStamp. I have to make a report and show the success and failure count for dynamically given ifows. So, to achieve this first i need to fetch MPL records based on time stamp. There are more than 1000 records.

HELP. So, Please let me know how can i achieve this to fetch high data records.

I am attaching the Iflow snips.  

SAP HANA service for SAP BTP SAP Cloud Platform, industry edition, client libraries OData SAP Integration Suite

entry.title: Issue with API_MAINTORDERCONFIRMATION via APIM Compared to Z BAPI
entry.content:
Hello Community,

I'm currently working on integrating maintenance order confirmations through the standard OData service API_MAINTORDERCONFIRMATION. Previously, we used a custom BAPI (ZF_G_EAM_WEP_CONF_CREATE) with a working payload that performed as expected when tested directly.

However, when we tried to use the same payload structure through the OData service via SAP API Management (APIM), we encountered issues.

Findings during testing:

  • The to_LongText field only accepts null values — any string value results in a failure.

  • The WorkNotCompleted field does not accept boolean values like true or false. It only works with numeric values.

  • Even after removing these two fields, we are still getting the following error:

    "Confirmation no. 0020912981 does not correspond to operation 0020"

Additional context:

We are transitioning from our custom Z BAPI to the standard OData service for a cleaner, more maintainable integration. The Z BAPI does accept to_LongText as a string and WorkNotCompleted as 'X' (character). These discrepancies between the two interfaces are causing unexpected issues.

Has anyone faced similar problems with API_MAINTORDERCONFIRMATION? Any guidance on handling to_LongText and WorkNotCompleted fields properly in the OData payload would be appreciated.

The following is a payload I am using(POST) : {
"MaintOrderConf": "209012981",
"MaintenanceOrder": "4005892228",
"MaintenanceOrderOperation": "0020",
"MaintenanceOrderSubOperation": "null",
"MaintenanceWorkCenter": "S16d1Ab",
"MaintenanceWorkCenterPlant": "K000",
"PersonnelNumber": "",
"IsFinalConfirmation": false,
"RemainingWorkQuantityUnit": false,
"ActualWorkQuantity": "3",
"ActualWorkQuantityUnit": "D",
"ActivityType": "",
"OperationConfirmedStartDate": "/Date(1744329600000)/",
"OperationConfirmedStartTime": "/Date(1744329600000)/",
"OperationConfirmedEndDate": "PT09H26M00S",
"OperationConfirmedEndTime": "PT09H26M00S",
"PostingDate": "/Date(1744329600000)/",
"ActualDuration": "null",
"ActualDurationUnit": "D,
"ConfirmationText": "Confirmed_By: Sza (3) s",
"to_LongText": " : "Replaced the filter unit as per standard operating procedure. No abnormalities were detected.",
"VarianceReasonCode": null
}

Thanks in advance!
Rofhiwa

entry.title: Batch at PO component level is not updated/assigned using SAP standard API for Purchase Order
entry.content: Using API_PURCHASEORDER_PROCESS_SRV Batch is not getting updated at PO Subcontracting level.

entry.title: Fiori Elements + RAP: How to preserve order of batch action calls? Wrong Batch order.
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I'm working on a Fiori Elements app based on RAP (ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model).
The user scans equipment one by one and then triggers a Print Label action.
The labels should be printed in the exact order the equipment was scanned, but sometimes they are printed in the wrong order.

What I observed:

  • The action printLabel is defined in the behavior definition.

  • When multiple rows are selected and the action is triggered, the backend method is called multiple times — once for each entity.

  • Although the UI sends a $batch, the printLabel method is executed separately per entity, and not necessarily in order.

This results in labels being printed in the wrong sequence, which causes confusion and extra manual effort.

My Behavior definition:

managed implementation in class ZBP_I_Equipment
strict(2);

define behavior for Z_I_Equipment alias Equipment
persistent table I_Equipment
lock master
{
    field( readonly ) Equipment;
    action printLabel;
}

 

My questions:

  1. Is there a way to ensure that printLabel is called once with all selected entries, preserving their original selection order?

  2. Is it possible to change the behavior to support multiple processing (e.g. on entity ... multiple) in this RAP setup?

  3. Are there best practices for preserving processing order in such scenarios?

Any guidance or working examples would be greatly appreciated.
Thank you!

entry.title: Fiori APP / Odata V2 to API
entry.content:
I have created a FIORI App with classical odata V2.

I'd like to use API data with Odata by using CPI

Instead of 
APP (odata) -> destination back-end 
APP (OData) -> Destination CPI -> Use API

I suppose this is possible but I don't know exactly how to do that.

I have 2 sub-account. One for the APP, One for Integration Suite. 

Do you know steps to acheive that ? Ideally with an example.

Thanks gurus for your pov on this little challenge

entry.title: A_SALESQUOTATION Odata Adapter
entry.content:
Daten im Odata-Service A_SALESQUOTATION liefern nicht die aktuellen Daten !!!

 

 

 

 

Erstes Angebot/Trace war ohne Opportunity-ID -> das passt soweit

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Um 10:21:25 wurde dem Angebot eine Opportunity hinzugefügt

 

 

Leider liefert aber der OData-Service die ID nicht. Es scheint so, als wären die Daten noch nicht in A_SALESQUOTATION angekommen

Es werden Daten zur Uhrzeit (UTC) vom alten Stand zurückgeliefert

entry.content:
I have created an OData service in Eclipse(It is not present in /IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE) and I was trying to connect it to my Fiori App in VS Code. However, I encountered the following error:

VS Code error:

Browser error when trying to access the HTTPS URL:

entry.title: How do I consume/display the data of Odata on Fiori App?
entry.content:
How do I consume/display the OData on my Fiori App? I've run my fiori app in local

Result in Gateway Client in SAP GUI:

 

Result in Fiori App:

I was expected sales document number instead of Chang_121 and user instead of Changed_121.

entry.title: How to send a high priority mail as in outlook from ABAP
entry.content:
Hi,

I am trying to send  a high priority mail from ABAP OData. I am currently using cl_bcs class. I have tried setting the priority in SODOCCHGI1 as 1. But still i am not able to receive mail with high importance flag.

entry.title: Creating Sales Orders with Billing Plans and Delivery Dates via API
entry.content:
While working on an integration to create sales orders in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, I came across two relevant OData APIs provided by SAP:

  • OData V4: CE_SALESORDER_0001

  • OData V2: API_SALES_ORDER_SRV

I encountered some unexpected limitations and I am hoping you could help me with a best practice approach. 

The Problem

Goal:

  • Create a Sales Order via API

  • Include a billing plan on item level

  • Include the requested delivery date on item level

What I Found

V2 – API_SALES_ORDER_SRV

  • Supports billing plan at item level 

  • Does not support Requested Delivery Date at item level directly in the item payload 

    • Strangely enough, the “Create Sales Order” Fiori app does let you enter requested delivery dates per item manually

    • You can only set it via a second call to the schedule line entity (to_ItemScheduleLine)

V4 – CE_SALESORDER_0001

  • No support for billing plans 

  • Does support Requested Delivery Date per item

The Workaround I’m Using

Since I want both the billing plan and requested delivery date, I’m forced to:

  1. Use V2 API to create the Sales Order with Billing Plan.

  2. Make a second API call to /A_SalesOrderItemScheduleLine to update the RequestedDeliveryDate.

My Questions

  • Why is OData V4, a newer version, missing such key functionality like billing plans?
  • Is there a reason why requested delivery date is not available directly in the item payload in V2?
  • Are there other options or best practices to handle this more efficiently?
  • Is there a roadmap for V2? Or will changes only apply for newer versions?

entry.title: Issue in Creating SAP Fiori Elements List Report Using Parameterized CDS View using OData V4 UI
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

We are currently developing a Fiori Elements List Report based on a parameterized CDS view in RAP. We have created a parameterized consumption CDS view and binding it using an OData V4 UI service. 

While generating the Fiori Elements List Report application, only the parameters are visible in the App as output fields, and no filters are available. While executing it, we are getting the below error.

 

Below is the CDS View which is created. The parameters are exposed from the base level CDS

@AbapCatalog.viewEnhancementCategory: [#NONE]
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'Consumption View for Proj Fin Report'
@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: true
@ObjectModel.usageType:{
    serviceQuality: #X,
    sizeCategory: #S,
    dataClass: #MIXED
}
@Metadata.allowExtensions: true
@VDM.viewType: #CONSUMPTION
define view entity ZPTD_C_PROJ_FIN_REP
  with parameters
    p_bukrs      : bukrs,
    p_perio_from : co_perio,
    p_perio_to   : co_perio,
    p_gjahr_from : gjahr,
    p_gjahr_to   : gjahr,
    p_pc1        : versn,
    p_pc2        : versn
  as select from ZPTD_I_PROJ_FIN_REP( p_bukrs:$parameters.p_bukrs,
                                      p_perio_from:$parameters.p_perio_from ,
                                      p_perio_to:$parameters.p_perio_to ,
                                      p_gjahr_from:$parameters.p_gjahr_from ,
                                      p_gjahr_to:$parameters.p_gjahr_to,
                                      p_pc1:$parameters.p_pc1 ,
                                      p_pc2:$parameters.p_pc2  )
{

While creating an OData V2 service, it is working, and the report is showing all the filters and data is displayed.

Is there any workaround to achieve this using OData V4? We are on S4HANA OP2023 version.

Thanks,

Faiz

entry.title: SAP Std API Odata V2 Consumption via Service Consumption Model
entry.content:
Hi Experts, 

I am in an "SAP S/4 Private cloud (10/2024) without BTP". Focus is clean core. For a given Standard Odata V2 API ( eg Business Partner A2X) how to consume this API?

1: Typical way through SEGW & Service implementation is discouraged as it does not support cleancore . To keep it simple , I dont want to implement anything in Tier3. 

2: I cannot consume it via Service Consumption model as this supports only V4. Moreover, I dont even the see the option of Odata during Service consumption model generation.  Creating Service Consumption Model | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: CI odata adapter: Edm.Guid 'Wrong literal format for literal'
entry.content:
Hello,

 

I'm building an iFlow using oData adapters (v2) in integration suite. When trying to update (PUT) the record adapter returns error:

com.sap.it.rt.adapter.http.api.exception.HttpResponseException: An internal server error occured: Wrong literal format for literal: 'f5cc9649-59ab-1[...]'

I tried to workaround this problem by:

  • using batch (same error);
  • changing local edmx file and GUID type field from Edm.Guid to Edm.String and using guid'f5cc9649-59ab-1[...]' (remote validation fails with Cannot convert guid'f5cc9649-...' to java.util.UUID
  • Playing with Resource Path in adapter's config - changing it to ${property.path} and setting hardcoded value: entityName(GuID=guid'f292992-aaaa-...') but same metadata validation

GUID is defined as:

<Key>
<PropertyRef Name="GuID"/>
</Key>
<Property Name="GuID" Type="Edm.Guid" Nullable="false" sap:display-format="UpperCase" sap:label="UUID" sap:quickinfo="16-byte UID in 32 chars (hexadecimal)" sap:updatable="false"/>

And using it manually (postman) is possible and works. I found that CI produces incorrect requests (in batch), skiping 'guid' part in key definition. Example batch requests, looks like this:

PUT entityName(GuID='f5cc9649-59ab-1[...]') HTTP/1.1

 instead of (which would work):

PUT entityName(GuID=guid'f5cc9649-59ab-1[...]') HTTP/1.1

So either key validation fails in adapter or (with batch) in backend. How to make it work? I tried sap.support and adapter's configuration but there's nothing about such problems. 

Here's my payload I'm testing with:

<entitySetName>
<entityName>
<GuID>f5cc9649-59ab-1[...]</GuID>
<ProcessedAt>1990-01-26T06:40:03.02</ProcessedAt>
</entityName>
</entitySetName>

And adapter's config:
